message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "Middle School",
    "grade": "8",
    "stream": "General",
    "academic_year": "",
    "total_pages": "5"
  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": ""
    }
  ],
  "units": [
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U1",
      "unit_name": "Life Processes and Heredity (Implicit Unit based on CG 3)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition, transport, exchange of materials, and reproduction",
        "C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals",
        "Transport of water in plants; circulation in animals",
        "Respiration and excretion",
        "Reproduction",
        "DNA, genes, chromosomes",
        "Changes in the sequence of DNA"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Life Processes",
        "Heredity",
        "Variation",
        "Nutrition",
        "Transport",
        "Exchange of Materials"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U2",
      "unit_name": "Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play to classify them into five kingdoms",
        "C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)",
        "C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level",
        "C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level",
        "C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Cellular diversity",
        "Ecological role (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition)",
        "Five kingdoms classification",
        "Levels of organisation (molecules to organisms)",
        "Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)",
        "Interactions at each level",
        "Mendel's laws",
        "Population level inheritance",
        "Natural selection",
        "Allele frequency changes",
        "Structure and function of organisms"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Organisms and Environment",
        "Cellular Diversity",
        "Classification",
        "Levels of Organisation",
        "Ecosystems",
        "Mendel's Laws",
        "Biological Evolution",
        "Natural Selection"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U3",
      "unit_name": "Interdisciplinary Connections (Implicit Unit based on CG 5)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science",
        "C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics",
        "C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Literature and arts influence on Science",
        "Case studies: Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, atomic bomb, green revolution, GMOs, conservation of biodiversity",
        "Social Sciences and Ethics perspective",
        "Sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music",
        "Use of muscles in dance form and sports"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Interdisciplinary Connections",
        "Science and Arts",
        "Ethics in Science",
        "Case Studies",
        "Scientific Principles in other subjects"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U4",
      "unit_name": "Indian Contributions to Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 6)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 6.1 – Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Significant contributions of India",
        "Concepts, explanations, methods",
        "Integrated manner"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Indian Contributions",
        "History of Science",
        "Integrated Curriculum"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U5",
      "unit_name": "Current Discoveries and Frontiers (Implicit Unit based on CG 7)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 7.1 – States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied",
        "C 7.2 – States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Most current discoveries and ideas",
        "Frontiers in scientific knowledge",
        "Science as ever evolving",
        "Unanswered questions",
        "Conceptual understanding appropriate to developmental stage"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Current Discoveries",
        "Scientific Frontiers",
        "Evolving Science",
        "Scientific Questions"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Middle School",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U6",
      "unit_name": "Nature of Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 8)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "C 8.1 – Develops accurate and appropriate models to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Geometric models",
        "Mathematical models",
        "Graphical models",
        "Manipulating variables",
        "Predicting results"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Nature of Science",
        "Scientific Modeling",
        "Real-life events",
        "Phenomena representation",
        "Variable manipulation"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "chapters": []
}
